design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate large interconnected environments, collect power-ups (grappling gun), solve environmental puzzles to access new areas, combat enemies in action sequences
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Exploration versus combat - balancing traversal through large environments with frequent enemy encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Batman using gadgets and combat skills to navigate Gotham's dangerous environments based on the 1992 film
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Action-adventure hybrid that prioritizes environmental exploration and gadget-based progression over pure beat-em-up combat; large level design encourages non-linear exploration paths

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-ups (grappling gun and others) serve as difficulty modulators by unlocking new traversal paths and combat options

progression.upgrade_system: Gadget-based progression: grappling gun and other power-ups collected from environments that enable access to previously unreachable areas
progression.unlock_mechanism: Power-up collection gates progression - grappling gun unlocks vertical and elevated passages in large environments
replayability_hooks: Multiple paths through large environments encourage different exploration routes
Power-up placement creates optional discovery moments
Combat encounters scale with player gadget acquisition

world_building_techniques: Film-licensed setting grounds narrative in 1992 Batman Returns movie universe
Environmental design reflects Gotham locations from source material
Large interconnected environments suggest urban exploration narrative

faction_indicators: Batman protagonist identity established through gadgets and Gotham setting
Enemy types likely include film-based villains and henchmen
danger_signals: High difficulty level signals challenging combat encounters
Large environments suggest potential for player disorientation
environmental_storytelling: Gotham urban environments reflect 1992 film aesthetic
Locked doors and vertical passages create environmental puzzle narrative
Power-up placement suggests Batman's resource scarcity and ingenuity themes

sources_used: Wikipedia - List of video games featuring Batman (Batman Returns Genesis entry)
